name: Reverse Wave
preceding_figure_names: Basic Weave, Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending, Closed Impetus and Feather Finish, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Feather Step, Hover Cross, Hover Feather, Natural Hover Telemark, Natural Telemark, Natural Twist Turn, Natural Weave, Natural Zig-Zag from PP, Open Telemark and Feather Ending, Open Telemark, Natural Turn, Outside Swivel and Feather Ending, Reverse Turn, Top Spin, Weave from PP
following_figure_names: Feather Step, Basic Weave (after step 4), Closed Impetus and Feather Finish (after step 6), Open Impetus (after step 6), Hover Feather (after step 8), Curved Feather to Back Feather (at corner)
notes:
There are five alignments of the Reverse Wave: Facing LOD 3/8 between 1-3, 1/8 between 4-6, 3/8 between 7-8 (or less at corner); Facing LOD 3/8 between 1-3, 1/4 between 4-6 to back DW of new LOD, 1/4 between 7-8; Facing DW 1/2 between 1-3, then continue as in alignment (1); Facing DW 1/2 between 1-3, then continue as in alignment (2); Facing DC 3/8 between 1-3, 1/4 between 4-6 to back the new LOD, 3/8 between 7-8. Alignments 2,4, and 5 are used to turn a corner.
Special Note -- Although the rise is taken from the T of LF on step 5, when the RF starts to move back for step 6 the R Heel must be in contact with the floor.
Extended Reverse Wave can be performed by adding SQQ after step 6: Man continues BLOD with TH, LF back, Q: T, RF back, R shoulder leading, Q: TH, LF back in CBMP; Lady continues facing LOD, poise not back weight, shoulder dancing in the back of the feet; then continue from step 4 again. Head can keep left or shape to R; feeling is legs dancing ahead of body.
Preceding figures include Basic Weave, Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending, Closed Impetus and Feather Finish, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Feather Step, Hover Cross, Hover Feather, Natural Hover Telemark, Natural Telemark, Natural Twist Turn, Natural Weave, Natural Zig-Zag from PP, Open Telemark and Feather Ending, Open Telemark, Natural Turn, Outside Swivel and Feather Ending, Reverse Turn, Top Spin, Weave from PP.
Following figures include Feather Step, Basic Weave (after step 4), Closed Impetus and Feather Finish (after step 6), Open Impetus (after step 6), Hover Feather (after step 8), Curved Feather to Back Feather (at corner).

comments: Rise at the end.
Not a very big step. Slightly different step from normal Reverse Turn because of the following figure (Reverse Wave).
Driving step.
Special Note -- Although the rise is taken from the T of LF on step 5, when the RF starts to move back for step 6 the R Heel must be in contact with the floor.
RF back, then keep head there.
Anchor R shoulder down. L side positive.
Lady heel turn, keep head, turning body underneath, effectively head is now shaped to right. Extend.
Slide fwd more.
Keep the shape to the R, head is to R. Shoulder rotated to R. L hip fwd/faster.
Enjoy this moment "longer", don't rush changing head to L.
Wave part: Use R leg, range of motion, curve with Man, stay with his right side. slowly turning head back smoothly.
LF fwd, Really push L hip diagonally forward (but not locked), side leading.
Head changed to shape to L.
After LF steps fwd, L shoulder connected to LF. change R shoulder forward at this time.
Heel lead. Really swing hip to R, to Man, use L leg under butt muscle.
R side body ribs to Man.
Head extends more.
Produce a curve starting from foot, leg and body, coming out from head. Use R leg, bring L leg underneath body. Head curve leftwards.
Drive with the leg, not upper body.
Head starting changing to L smoothly.
Think about head taking more "space" explore the space.
Enjoy the moment longer.
Head to L.
2nd part of Natural Turn finish. Could do Closed Impetus or Open Impetus here.
